description:
You are a backup-operator trainee who needs to validate that
    a tiny test archive can be backed-up and restored while
    showing the correct time-zone and locale settings in a
    two-line report file.

    Complete the following workflow from an interactive bash
    session (no super-user rights are required):

    1. Create the working directory
          /home/user/restore_test
       if it does not yet exist and make sure you stay inside it
       for the remainder of the exercise.

    2. Inside the directory, create a text file named
          /home/user/restore_test/original.txt
       with the *exact* single line of text (do not add a blank
       line before or after it):
          This is a sample backup file.

    3. Produce a compressed tar archive named
          /home/user/restore_test/backup.tgz
       that contains the file original.txt while *excluding*
       every other file or directory.  Use reproducible
       (deterministic) options so that two consecutive archives
       would be byte-for-byte identical (e.g. set owner/group to
       0, strip access/mtime metadata, etc.).

    4. Immediately delete original.txt **only after** the archive
       has been safely created.

    5. With the environment variables
          TZ=UTC
          LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8
       create a timestamp for the fixed moment
          2024-03-01 12:00:00
       using the `date` command (do *not* rely on the host clock;
       explicitly pass the moment to `date -d` or `date --date`).
       Record this timestamp in the log file described in step 8.

    6. Untar backup.tgz so that original.txt is back on disk.

    7. Using the following environment variables
          TZ=Europe/Paris
          LC_ALL=fr_FR.UTF-8
       create a second timestamp for the fixed moment
          2024-03-01 13:00:00
       (again, compute it with `date -d` rather than the system
       clock).  Record this timestamp in the log file as well.

    8. Create a plain-text log file
          /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
       that contains *exactly two lines* in the precise format
       shown below (no leading/trailing spaces, tabs, or extra
       newlines).  The automated test will compare it byte by
       byte, so be very strict:

          [UTC][en_US.UTF-8] 2024-03-01 12:00:00 Backup archive backup.tgz created
          [Europe/Paris][fr_FR.UTF-8] 2024-03-01 13:00:00 Restore of original.txt verified OK

       Notes on the required format:
       • Square brackets around the time-zone string **and then**
         around the locale.
       • A single space separates each logical field.
       • All colons in the timestamps are literal ‘:’ characters.
       • The first line must mention “backup.tgz created”.
       • The second line must mention “original.txt verified OK”.

    9. Verification criteria (what the automated grader will
       check):
       • /home/user/restore_test/backup.tgz exists and is a valid
         gzipped tar archive containing exactly one member named
         “original.txt”.
       • /home/user/restore_test/original.txt exists **after** the
         restore step and its contents are the single line
         indicated in step 2.
       • /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log exists and
         matches the two-line format shown in step 8 *verbatim*.

    10. You may use any combination of ordinary shell commands
        (`echo`, `printf`, `tar`, `date`, `touch`, `rm`, etc.).
        Parallel or chained execution with operators such as
        `&&`, `||`, `;`, or subshells is welcome, but make sure
        the final state described above is obtained.

    When everything is finished, you should be able to run

        cat /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log

    and see exactly the two lines described in step 8.
truth:
The automated test suite will confirm that the final system
    state contains the following files with these properties:

    1. Path: /home/user/restore_test/original.txt
       Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
       Contents (17 bytes + LF): “This is a sample backup file.\n”

    2. Path: /home/user/restore_test/backup.tgz
       • Must exist.
       • Must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
       • When extracted, must yield exactly one file named
         “original.txt” whose byte content is identical to item 1.

    3. Path: /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
       Permissions: 0644
       Exact byte-for-byte content (including the terminating
       newline after the second line):

       “[UTC][en_US.UTF-8] 2024-03-01 12:00:00 Backup archive backup.tgz created\n”
       “[Europe/Paris][fr_FR.UTF-8] 2024-03-01 13:00:00 Restore of original.txt verified OK\n”

    The grader will additionally compute SHA-256 digests of both
    original.txt copies (inside and outside the archive) and
    assert that the digests are equal.

    No other files are required for verification.
